notes: Production-tree (--omit=dev) high/critical advisories that are accepted with rationale. The Dependency Audit workflow fails any production high/critical NOT listed here. Each entry must include a rationale and review_by date. When upstream issues a fix, remove the entry; if the rationale ages out, escalate.

allowed_advisories.title: bigint-buffer Vulnerable to Buffer Overflow via toBigIntLE() Function
allowed_advisories.rationale: Transitive of @solana/web3.js. The vulnerable toBigIntLE() requires attacker-controlled binary input; our use is server-side decoding of trusted Solana RPC responses (no user-supplied buffers reach this path). Upstream maintainer has not published a fix at the time of triage.

allowed_advisories.title: path-to-regexp outputs backtracking regular expressions
allowed_advisories.rationale: Transitive only via @vercel/node and @vercel/remix-builder, which are part of the Vercel CLI build chain pulled in by @cloudflare/next-on-pages. Not bundled into the production Next.js runtime served from Vercel.

allowed_advisories.title: Picomatch has a ReDoS vulnerability via extglob quantifiers
allowed_advisories.rationale: Build-time glob matcher. Used by Rollup/Vercel build tooling, never receives user input at runtime.

allowed_advisories.title: Rollup 4 has Arbitrary File Write via Path Traversal
allowed_advisories.rationale: Build-time bundler. Exploitation requires processing untrusted Rollup config / plugin inputs; our pipeline only feeds it our own source.

allowed_advisories.title: minimatch ReDoS: nested *() extglobs generate catastrophically backtracking
allowed_advisories.rationale: Build-time pattern matcher. Same shape as picomatch - never sees runtime user input.

allowed_advisories.title: minimatch ReDoS via repeated wildcards with non-matching literal
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74.

allowed_advisories.title: minimatch ReDoS: matchOne() combinatorial backtracking via multiple non-adjacent GLOBSTAR segments
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74.

allowed_advisories.title: node-tar Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Creation/Overwrite via Hardlink Path Traversal
allowed_advisories.rationale: Transitive of @vercel/fun (Vercel CLI build helper). Not bundled into runtime; tar.extract is never called against attacker-controlled archives in our deploy.

allowed_advisories.title: Arbitrary File Read/Write via Hardlink Target Escape Through Symlink Chain
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-34x7-hfp2-rc4v.

allowed_advisories.title: node-tar Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Overwrite and Symlink Poisoning via Insufficient Path Sanitization
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-34x7-hfp2-rc4v.

allowed_advisories.title: node-tar Symlink Path Traversal via Drive-Relative Linkpath
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-34x7-hfp2-rc4v. Drive-relative linkpath only applicable on Windows extraction; Vercel CLI runs on Linux.

allowed_advisories.title: tar has Hardlink Path Traversal via Drive-Relative Linkpath
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-34x7-hfp2-rc4v.

allowed_advisories.title: Race Condition in node-tar Path Reservations via Unicode Ligature Collisions
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-34x7-hfp2-rc4v.

allowed_advisories.title: Undici: Malicious WebSocket 64-bit length overflows parser and crashes process
allowed_advisories.rationale: Transitive via @vercel/* and miniflare. Both are dev/build tools; the production worker uses Cloudflare's native fetch, not undici.

allowed_advisories.title: Undici has Unhandled Exception in WebSocket Client Due to Invalid server response
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-f269-vfmq-vjvj.

allowed_advisories.title: Undici has Unbounded Memory Consumption in WebSocket permessage-deflate
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same surface as GHSA-f269-vfmq-vjvj.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js HTTP request deserialization can lead to DoS when using insecure React Server Components
allowed_advisories.rationale: Affects self-hosted Next.js deployments. Our app runs on Vercel's managed platform which mitigates the issue at the edge. We pin Next 15.x; will upgrade as soon as a fixed minor lands.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js has a Denial of Service with Server Components
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same deployment-shape mitigation as GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js Vulnerable to Denial of Service with Server Components
allowed_advisories.rationale: Newly disclosed DoS via Server Components. Vercel managed platform applies edge-level mitigations. No stable fixed version available across 15.x or 16.x stable at triage (affected range spans to 16.3.0-canary.5). Will upgrade when upstream publishes a stable fix.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js vulnerable to Denial of Service via connection exhaustion in applications using Cache Components
allowed_advisories.rationale: DoS via Cache Components connection exhaustion. Pre-launch with no live traffic; Vercel managed platform applies rate-limiting at edge. No stable fixed version at triage. Will upgrade when upstream publishes a stable fix.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js vulnerable to server-side request forgery in applications using WebSocket upgrades
allowed_advisories.rationale: SSRF via WebSocket upgrade path. Our middleware.ts does not handle WebSocket upgrades; all external traffic goes through Cloudflare Workers, not Next.js middleware. No stable fixed version at triage.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass through dynamic route parameter injection
allowed_advisories.rationale: Middleware bypass via dynamic route params. Our middleware.ts performs CSRF origin validation; actual authentication (JWT/session) is enforced by the Cloudflare Worker API via httpOnly cookies — a Next.js middleware bypass does not grant authenticated access to protected resources. No stable fixed version at triage.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in App Router applications via segment-prefetch routes
allowed_advisories.rationale: Same defense-in-depth argument as GHSA-492v-c6pp-mqqv: CSRF bypass does not bypass CF Worker auth. No stable fixed version at triage.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in Pages Router applications using i18n
allowed_advisories.rationale: Bypass requires Pages Router i18n configuration. This app uses App Router exclusively with no i18n config — the vulnerable code path is not reachable.

allowed_advisories.title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in App Router applications via segment-prefetch routes - Incomplete Fix Follow-Up
allowed_advisories.rationale: Incomplete fix for GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f. Same defense-in-depth argument applies: CF Worker auth is the enforcement layer, not Next.js middleware. No stable fixed version at triage.
